title: task
description: routing.rs:80-87 - NeighborTable::update eviction uses min_by_key(|_, t| *t) to find oldest entry, but this fails after u32 timestamp wraparound (~49 days). Example: Entry A at last_seen_ms=0xFFFFFF00 (old, before wrap) vs Entry B at last_seen_ms=0x00000100 (recent, after wrap). B's raw value is smaller so it gets evicted despite being newer. Fix: use wrapping_sub comparison like prune() does. Failure scenario: After 49+ days uptime with full neighbor table, newest neighbor is evicted instead of oldest.
close_reason: Fixed: Added explicit tie-break by slot index to NeighborTable eviction. Changed min_by_key from (*t) to (*t, *i) for deterministic behavior when timestamps match.
